#Muuqaal Burco – Mujaahidad Dhudi, oo ka mid ahayd 12-kii hablood ee geesiyaasha ahaa ee ka tirsanaa halgankii SNM, kuwaas oo Jimcihii 27-05-1988 si taariikhi ah ula galay magaalada Burco, ayaa maanta si rasmi ah loogu dalacsiiyay darajada Gaashaanle Sare, taas oo muujinaysa aqoonsi qaran iyo qaddarin ay mudnayd muddo dheer.

Mujaahidad Dhudi waxay ka mid ahayd haweenkii safka hore kaga jiray halgankii dib-u-xoraynta, xilli ay adkayd in gabdho ay qaataan kaalmo ciidan iyo mid halgan. Waxay door muuqda ka qaadatay halganka, iyada oo muujisay geesinimo, adkaysi iyo waddaniyad aan la ilaawi karin.

Dalacsiintan maanta la siiyay ayaa ah mid taariikhi ah, isla markaana fariin cad u diraysa bulshada, gaar ahaan kuwa yidhaahda “hablayagu ciyaar ciyaar mood”, maadaama ay caddeynayso in haweenku ay yihiin tiir muhiim u ah difaaca, xorriyadda iyo dhismaha qaranka.

Tallaabadan ayaa sidoo kale dhiirrigelin u noqonaysa gabdhaha iyo haweenka Somaliland , iyadoo muujinaysa in dadaalka, halganka iyo u adeegidda qaranku ay mar walba leeyihiin abaal iyo aqoonsi, xitaa haddii uu dib u dhaco.

Ugu dambayn, dalacsiinta Mujaahidad Dhudi waa xasuusin taariikheed oo muujinaysa in halgankii SNM uusan ahayn mid rag oo keliya, balse ay haween geesiyaal ahi laf-dhabar u ahaayeen guusha la gaadhay.

How to Conform to a MAGA World

Last week and again on Sunday during the Super Bowl, people were screaming online about Bad Bunny performing. “This is America’s football,” they said. Fine. Then let’s talk about America.

Puerto Rico is a territory of the United States. Puerto Ricans are American citizens. That is not an opinion. That is a fact. So what exactly is the complaint here. That they speak Spanish. That they are brown. That their culture does not fit someone else’s idea of what America should look like.

It got so ridiculous that Turning Point USA decided to put on an alternate show. Their version of America. On stage stood Kid Rock, lip syncing so badly the music was not even aligned. Around him stood a carefully curated image of what they believe America should be. No diversity. No culture outside their own. No room for anyone who looks or sounds different.

So let’s ask the real question. What should America be. A land of immigrants. A land of freedom. A land where democracy matters more than skin color or accents. Or is the MAGA vision this. If you are brown skinned, speak with an accent, or come from a culture they do not like, you are not welcome.

And we do not have to guess what that vision is. Donald Trump has said it out loud. Repeatedly. He has asked why the United States cannot get more immigrants from Europe. At rallies and fundraisers he has talked about wanting immigrants from “nice” countries like Norway, Sweden, Denmark, and Switzerland. He has contrasted those countries with Somalia, Afghanistan, and Haiti, calling them disastrous, filthy, and disgusting. He has openly referenced his own past remarks about so called “shit hole countries” and never walked them back.

At the same time, his administration explored changes to the refugee system that would prioritize white Christians from South Africa and parts of Europe while shutting the door on most other asylum seekers. That is not accidental language. That is a pattern. And when challenged, he briefly claimed he wanted immigrants from “everywhere, everywhere,” a statement that directly contradicts the policies and rhetoric that followed.

This is not about borders. This is about race. Critics have pointed out what is obvious to anyone paying attention. When immigrants from non white, developing nations are singled out as dangerous or undesirable while immigrants from predominantly white European countries are praised as preferable, that aligns with white nationalist thinking. Whether people want to admit it or not.

I served in the military with Puerto Ricans. Some of the best sergeants and officers I ever knew. I loved their food. I loved their culture. I trusted them with my life. They are just as American as you and I.

Now let’s talk about what is not American. This administration’s use of Immigration and Customs Enforcement as an overforce arm of intimidation. Masked agents grabbing people off the street because of how they look or how they speak. A president of the United States posting images depicting a former president as monkeys because they were Black and then never truly apologizing. And a Republican Party that responded not with outrage but with praise. Good job for taking it down they said.

That is not leadership. That is cowardice. And no, this is not just opinion. It is history. From his first term to his second, the record is full of racist comments and racist policies. Again and again.

This is not the America I grew up with. This is not a president who respects people unless they fit a narrow definition of whiteness. Think about this. White South Africans are suddenly offered asylum for so called persecution, a claim that has been repeatedly proven false, while families who have lived here for decades are treated like criminals. Start there.

That is the MAGA world. And it is trying to tell millions of Americans that they do not belong.

I do belong to this country. I served it. I defended it. And I will not stay silent while it is twisted into something unrecognizable.

Paul
Veteran. Business Owner. Defender of the Constitution.

A Proud Legacy of Service

David Ronald Paris, MMM, MB, CD, was born in New Glasgow, Nova Scotia, in September 1951. Like his father, Leonard Paris Sr., and his eldest brother, Leonard Paris Jr., David chose a life of service to Canada. At just 16 years old, he joined the reserve unit of the First Battalion Nova Scotia Highlanders. He remained with the unit for five years, becoming a junior non‑commissioned officer and beginning what would become a remarkable military career.

In 1972, David joined the regular Canadian army. Over the next three decades, he rose steadily through the ranks, from Private to Master Warrant Officer. In 1975, he joined the Canadian Airborne Regiment, where he earned his Parachute Wings. His career took him across the world, with postings in Germany, Norway, Cyprus, Syria, Somalia, Albania, and Israel. Within Canada, he served in Edmonton, Petawawa, and Ottawa.

David’s courage and dedication were recognized through numerous medals and awards. Among them was the Medal of Bravery, awarded for saving the lives of two people after a gas explosion in their home. In 1997, he was appointed to the Order of Military Merit, one of Canada’s highest military honours, recognizing his exceptional service and leadership.

After retiring from the regular force in 2001, David continued to serve in both regular and reserve duties in Edmonton, Alberta. Altogether, his career spanned an incredible forty‑four years. His lifelong commitment reflects not only personal achievement but also the strength of a family tradition rooted in service to Canada.

David Ronald Paris’s story is one of courage, discipline, and devotion. It shows how one individual—and one family—can make a lasting impact on their country. For students today, his example is a reminder that service, whether in the military or in community life, is a powerful way to build pride and legacy.

உலகிலேயே சிறந்த பயணிக்கான கின்னஸ் சாதனையைப் படைத்தவர் சார்லஸ் வீலி (Charles Veley). இவர் ஒரு மிகப்பெரும் கோடீஸ்வரர்; இவருக்குப் பணம் ஒரு பொருட்டே இல்லை. உலகிலேயே செல்வதற்கு மிகவும் கடினமான இடங்கள் என வகைப்படுத்தப்பட்டுள்ள 973 பகுதிகளுக்கும் சென்றதால், "உலகின் மிகச் சிறந்த பயணி" என கின்னஸ் புத்தகத்தால் இவர் அங்கீகரிக்கப்பட்டார்.

அவர் சென்ற சில அபூர்வமான பகுதிகளின் பட்டியல்:

ராக்-ஆல் (Rockall): வட அட்லாண்டிக் கடலில் உள்ள ஒரு சிறிய பாறை. இதில் ஏறுவது மிகக் கடினம். சார்லஸ் அலைகளுக்கு இடையே நீந்திச் சென்று அந்தப் பாறையைத் தொட்டுச் சாதனை படைத்தார்.

ஆயில் ராக்ஸ் (Oil Rocks): காஸ்பியன் கடலின் நடுவே உள்ள ஒரு பிரம்மாண்டமான எண்ணெய் கிணறு நகரம். இங்கு செல்வதற்கு ராணுவ ஹெலிகாப்டர் அனுமதியைப் பெற்றுச் சென்றார்.

சார்லஸ் வீலி வெறும் நாடுகளை மட்டும் பார்ப்பதில்லை; வரைபடத்தில் உள்ள ஒவ்வொரு சிறிய புள்ளியையும் நேரில் பார்ப்பதையே தனது லட்சியமாகக் கொண்டுள்ளார். இப்படி உலகின் அனைத்து நாடுகள், அவற்றின் மாநிலங்கள், யுனெஸ்கோ அங்கீகரித்த இடங்கள், சென்றடைவதற்கே கடினமான தீவுகள் மற்றும் உலகின் புகழ்பெற்ற உணவகங்கள் என அவரது சாதனைப் பட்டியல் நீள்கிறது. 

அவரையே சவாலுக்கு இழுத்த ஒரு இடம் நார்வே நாட்டிற்குச் சொந்தமான புவே தீவு (Bouvet Island). இத்தீவு தென் துருவத்திற்கும் ஆப்பிரிக்காவிற்கும் நடுவே தனித்து விடப்பட்டுள்ளது. 1927-ல் நார்வேயிலிருந்து சென்ற சிலர் அங்கு கொடியை நட்டதோடு சரி, அதன்பின் யாரும் அங்கே சென்றதில்லை. அந்தத் தீவை மையமாக வைத்து 2,500 கி.மீ. சுற்றளவிற்கு ஒரு வட்டம் வரைந்தால், அதற்குள் வேறு எந்த நிலப்பரப்போ, தீவோ அல்லது மனித நடமாட்டமோ கிடையாது.

அங்கே ஒரு மனிதர் கூட இல்லை; ஒரு மரம், செடி, கொடி கூட வளராது. தீவு முழுவதும் மைல் கணக்கான தடிமனில் பனிப்பாறைகள் (Glacier) படர்ந்துள்ளன. தரை இறங்குவதற்கு கடற்கரைகள் கிடையாது, செங்குத்தான பாறைகள் வழியாகத்தான் ஏற வேண்டும். ஆயிரக்கணக்கான பெங்குவின்கள் மட்டுமே அங்கு வசிக்கின்றன. விண்வெளிக்குச் சென்றவர்களின் எண்ணிக்கையை விட இத்தீவிற்குச் சென்றவர்களின் எண்ணிக்கை மிகவும் குறைவு.

அங்கு செல்ல வழக்கமான கப்பலோ, விமானமோ கிடையாது. பெரும் முயற்சிக்குப் பிறகு, தென் ஆப்பிரிக்காவிலிருந்து அண்டார்டிகா செல்லும் ஆய்வுக் கப்பல் ஒன்றில் ஹெலிகாப்டருடன் பயணமானார். நார்வே அரசிடம் அனுமதி கேட்டபோது, "அங்கே சென்று சாகப் போகிறாயா?" என்பது போலப் பார்த்துவிட்டு அனுமதி அளித்தனர்.

கப்பல் தீவை நெருங்கியதும் ஹெலிகாப்டர் மூலம் தீவை அடைந்தார். தரையிறங்க ஒரே ஒரு இடம் மட்டுமே இருந்தது. சார்லஸின் அதிர்ஷ்டம், அன்று காற்று சாதகமாக இருந்தது. தீவில் இறங்கி, செல்ஃபி எடுத்துத் தனது பயணத்தை உறுதி செய்தார். அந்தச் சில நிமிடப் பயணத்திற்காக அவர் செலவிட்டது பல லட்சம் டாலர்கள்.

சார்லஸ் வீலி இப்போது வரை சும்மா இருக்கவில்லை. 2026-ன் தற்போதைய நிலவரப்படி, அவர் தனது பயணப் பட்டியலில் 1,417 இடங்களை எட்டிப் பிடித்து, 'உலகின் அதிகம் பயணித்த மனிதர்கள்' (Most Traveled People - MTP) தரவரிசையில் முதலிடத்தில் நீடிக்கிறார்.

சமீபத்தில் அவர் மேற்கொண்ட சில அதிரடி சாகசங்கள்:

* அஜர்பைஜான் நாட்டில் போர் முடிந்து சமீபத்தில் மீட்கப்பட்ட அக்தாம் (Agdam) மற்றும் சூஷா (Shusha) போன்ற பகுதிகளுக்கு கண்ணிவெடி அபாயத்தையும் மீறிச் சென்றார்.

* சோமாலியாவின் அபெய் (Abyei) என்ற சர்ச்சைக்குரிய எல்லைப் பகுதிக்கும், எத்தியோப்பியா நாட்டின் உட்பகுதிகளுக்கும் பயணம் மேற்கொண்டார்.

* ஆஷ்மோர் மற்றும் கார்டியர் தீவுகள் (Ashmore and Cartier Islands), ஜார்விஸ் தீவு (Jarvis Island) மற்றும் கிங்மேன் ரீஃப் (Kingman Reef) போன்ற மிகச் சிறிய பசிபிக் கடல் பகுதிகளுக்கும் சென்று வந்துள்ளார்.

உங்களிடம் தாராளமாகப் பணமும், பயணத்தின் மீது அதீதக் காதலும் இருந்தால், உங்கள் போதையே உங்கள் தொழிலாக மாறிவிடும்

~ நியாண்டர் செல்வன்

#Centrafrique #ECOBANK Centrafrique : formation du comité de direction sur le leadership numérique et l'Intelligence Artificielle

Le comité de direction (CODIR) d'ECOBANK Centrafrique a entamé une formation de deux jours sur le thème "Leadership numérique et Intelligence Artificielle". Cette initiative vise à renforcer les compétences des dirigeants de la banque en matière de transformation digitale et d'IA.

Une formation assurée par #DHI_Centrafrique

DHI Centrafrique est une entreprise de services numériques (ESN) spécialisée en transformation digitale, Data & IA, cybersécurité, solutions ERP & GED et formation exécutive.
- La formation est dispensée par des experts du cabinet DHI Centrafrique.

Objectifs de la formation

- Renforcer les compétences des dirigeants d'ECOBANK Centrafrique en matière de leadership numérique et d'IA
- Accompagner la transformation digitale de la banque
- Améliorer la compétitivité de la banque sur le marché centrafricain

Cette formation témoigne de l'engagement d'ECOBANK Centrafrique à rester à la pointe de la technologie et à offrir des services innovants à ses clients.

Xawaaladaha; Dhiigga Dhaqaalaha iyo Saameynta Dalabka

Marka aynu ka soo degno daruuraha aragtiyaha reer Galbeedka oo aynu cagaha dhigno ciidda gaduudan ee dhulkeenna hooyo, arrintu si kale ayay u ekaanaysaa. Dhaqaalaha Soomaaliya ma aha mid ku socda buugagta ay qoreen rag dhintay qarni ka hor; waa mid nool, neefsanaya, mararka qaarna waalan, kaas oo ay hagayaan arrimo dhaqan iyo siyaasadeed oo innoo gaar ah.

Miyaad maqashay dhawaaqa “Biib-Biib” ee taleefankaaga ku soo dhacaya marka bishu dhammaad tahay? Waa farriinta Xawaaladda. Waa codka ugu macaan ee qoysas badan oo Soomaaliyeed ay sugaan, waana dhiigga dhabta ah ee ku jira xididdada dhaqaalaheenna.

Soomaaliya waxay ka mid tahay waddamada adduunka ugu tiirsan Xawaaladaha (Remittances). Warbixinnada bangiyada adduunku waxay sheegayaan in qiyaastii 17.7% dhaqaalaheenna guud (GDP) uu ka yimaado dibadda. Laakiin adiga iyo anigu uma baahnin Bankiga Adduunka inuu noo sheego taas; waan aragnaa maalin kasta. Lacagtan qurbaha ka timaadaa ma aha kaliya “Biil”; waa matoorka dhaqaajiya suuqa. Waa tan u beddesha riyada ah “Caawa hilib baan rabaa” (Rabitaan) una beddesha “Hilibkii waan soo iibsaday” (Dalab Wax-ku-ool ah). Haddii lacagtaas la waayo, suuqyada Bakaaraha iyo Suuq-Yare way istaagayaan. Waana sababta marka ay bisha Ramadaan soo gasho ama Ciiduhu soo dhowaadaan, aad arkayso qiimaha dharka iyo macmacaanka oo cirka isku shareeray. Ganacsatada Suuqa Xamar-Weyne ma aha faaliyeyaal, laakiin way ogyihiin in jeebka dadku buuxo xilligaas, sidaas darteedna ay tahay xilliga (Seasonality) ugu habboon ee la kordhin karo qiimaha.

Transparency International’s Corruption Perceptions Index 2025 paints a troubling global picture: corruption is worsening, leadership against it is weakening, and progress remains limited.

The global average score has fallen to 42 out of 100, the lowest in more than a decade, while over two-thirds of countries score below 50, showing widespread failure to control corruption. Even long-standing democracies are slipping, with declining scores in countries such as the United States (64), Canada (75), New Zealand (81), the United Kingdom (70), France (66), and Sweden (80, a sign that anti-corruption leadership is eroding where it was once strongest.

At the same time, civic space is shrinking in many parts of the world, undermining the fight against corruption. Since 2012, 36 of the 50 countries with major score declines have also restricted freedoms of expression, association, or assembly, making it harder for journalists, NGOs, and whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing. The consequences are severe: 150 journalists investigating corruption have been killed outside conflict zones over the past decade. Yet public frustration is rising, with Gen Z-led protests in countries such as Nepal and Madagascar demanding accountability, better services, and economic opportunity.

Despite the downturn, the index highlights stark contrasts and signs of hope. Denmark (89), Finland (88), and Singapore (84) remain the least corrupt countries, while South Sudan, Somalia, and Venezuela sit at the bottom with single-digit scores and heavily repressed civil societies. Long-term declines in places like Türkiye, Hungary, and Nicaragua point to systemic democratic backsliding, whereas improvements in Estonia, South Korea, and Seychellesshow that sustained reforms, strong oversight, and digital governance can reduce corruption. The overall message is clear: corruption is not inevitable, but without renewed political leadership and protection of civic freedoms, global progress will continue to stall.

Diaspora communities are powerhouses of change. 

An inspiring evening hosted by IOM UK and IOM - UN Migration bringing together UK-based Somali professionals and community leaders to highlight how diaspora-led initiatives are supporting locally owned projects in the Somali regions, from health and education to infrastructure.

We were pleased to represent RISE Projects as part of the panel discussion alongside Abdiaziz Arab, Safia Jama MBE and moderated by Killian Clifford, sharing learning from our work with IOM over the past two years on digital financial literacy. This has focused on supporting UK-based Somali communities to increase their understanding of digital remittances and crowdfunding platforms, helping strengthen collective giving and investment into locally led development.

A powerful reminder of the impact diaspora-led approaches can have when communities are informed, engaged, and supported

تحرك قانوني في لاهاي بعد يوم من استعراض القوات المصرية في الصومال:
أنصار “الملك فوزي ديرية” يطعنون في اتفاقيتي 1897 و1954… والقاهرة تتجه إلى تطويق عسكري استراتيجي
في تطور متسارع يعكس تشابك المسارات العسكرية والقانونية في القرن الإفريقي، أعلن أنصار جلالة الملك فوزي ديرية، اليوم، تقدمهم رسميًا بدعوى أمام محكمة العدل الدولية في لاهاي، للمطالبة بإلغاء اتفاقيتي عامي 1897 و1954 اللتين تم بموجبهما – بحسب وصفهم – “ضم الأراضي الصومالية إلى إثيوبيا عبر ترتيبات استعمارية بريطانية”.
وجاء هذا التحرك بعد يوم واحد فقط من اصطفاف القوات المصرية المشاركة ضمن بعثة الاتحاد الإفريقي لدعم الأمن والاستقرار في الصومال، في مشهد اعتبره مراقبون رسالة سياسية مزدوجة إلى أديس أبابا.

خلفية الاتفاقيتين: إرث استعماري محل نزاع

تعود اتفاقية 1897 إلى تفاهمات بين بريطانيا والإمبراطورية الإثيوبية في عهد الإمبراطور منليك الثاني، حيث أعادت لندن ترسيم الحدود بين “الصومال البريطاني” وإثيوبيا، مانحة الأخيرة مناطق واسعة يقطنها صوماليون.
أما اتفاقية 1954 فجاءت بعد إعادة ترتيب الوضع القانوني لإقليم أوجادين، حيث سلّمت بريطانيا – التي كانت تدير أجزاء من الإقليم – السيطرة الكاملة إلى إثيوبيا.
أنصار الملك فوزي يعتبرون أن:
الاتفاقيتين تمتا دون استشارة السكان المحليين.
الشعب الصومالي لم يكن طرفًا قانونيًا في أي منهما.
الاتفاقات الاستعمارية لا يمكن أن تشكل أساسًا دائمًا لسيادة دولة في ضوء تطور القانون الدولي ومبدأ تقرير المصير.
ويستند مقدمو الدعوى – وفق مصادر قريبة منهم – إلى مبادئ ميثاق الأمم المتحدة، وإلى سوابق قضائية في محكمة العدل الدولية تتعلق بحق الشعوب في تقرير مصيرها وبطلان بعض الترتيبات الاستعمارية.

التحرك إلى محكمة العدل الدولية: أبعاد قانونية معقدة

التحرك إلى لاهاي يفتح مسارًا قانونيًا بالغ التعقيد، إذ إن محكمة العدل الدولية لا تنظر في القضايا إلا بموافقة الدول المعنية أو بناءً على اختصاص سابق مقبول من الأطراف.
السؤال المحوري هنا:
هل ستقبل إثيوبيا الاختصاص القضائي للمحكمة في هذه القضية؟
خبراء قانون دولي يرون أن الدعوى – حتى لو لم تُقبل شكليًا – قد تحقق أهدافًا سياسية وإعلامية، أبرزها:
إعادة تدويل ملف أوجادين.
إحراج إثيوبيا دبلوماسيًا.
ربط النزاع الحدودي بمبدأ تقرير المصير بدلًا من اعتباره نزاعًا داخليًا.
القاهرة: بين خطاب حفظ السلام واستراتيجية التطويق
في القاهرة، لم يصدر تعليق رسمي مباشر على خطوة أنصار الملك فوزي، لكن مصادر سياسية أشارت إلى أن مصر “تتابع التطورات القانونية في الإقليم عن كثب”.
ويأتي ذلك في سياق انتشار عسكري مصري متصاعد في الصومال، شمل:
قوات برية ضمن بعثة الاتحاد الإفريقي.
طائرات هجوم أرضي ومروحيات دعم.
تعزيزات لوجستية ومدرعات.
هذا التمركز يُقرأ في أديس أبابا باعتباره جزءًا من استراتيجية “تطويق غير مباشر” لإثيوبيا، خاصة في ظل الخلافات المستمرة حول سد النهضة.
محللون يرون أن القاهرة تعتمد مقاربة متعددة المسارات:
المسار العسكري: تثبيت وجود في محيط إثيوبيا الجغرافي.
المسار الدبلوماسي: تعزيز التحالفات مع الصومال وجيبوتي.
المسار القانوني غير المباشر: دعم – ولو سياسيًا – أي تحرك يضعف الأساس القانوني لضم أوجادين.
رسالة سياسية مزدوجة
التزامن بين الاصطفاف العسكري المصري والتحرك القضائي لأنصار الملك فوزي لا يبدو – في نظر مراقبين – مجرد صدفة زمنية.
بل يُفهم كرسالة ذات شقين:
إلى أديس أبابا: أن الضغط لن يكون فقط عبر ملف المياه، بل عبر ملفات تاريخية وحدودية.
إلى الداخل الصومالي: أن خيار “المسار القانوني الدولي” بات مطروحًا إلى جانب العمل السياسي.
ردود الفعل المتوقعة في إثيوبيا
من المرجح أن تعتبر إثيوبيا الدعوى:
تدخلاً في سيادتها.
محاولة لتسييس القانون الدولي.
جزءًا من ضغوط إقليمية مرتبطة بملف سد النهضة.
وقد تتجه أديس أبابا إلى:
تحرك دبلوماسي مضاد داخل الاتحاد الإفريقي.
تعزيز خطاب “وحدة الأراضي الإثيوبية”.
اتهام أطراف إقليمية بدعم النزعات الانفصالية.
بين القانون والميدان: أي مسار سيغلب؟
القضية تضع القرن الإفريقي أمام معادلة دقيقة:
في لاهاي: جدل قانوني حول شرعية اتفاقات عمرها أكثر من قرن.
في الميدان: انتشار عسكري وتحالفات متغيرة.
في السياسة: إعادة تعريف لموازين القوى الإقليمية.
يبقى السؤال الأهم:
هل يتحول الطعن القانوني إلى ورقة ضغط تفاوضية، أم يفتح بابًا فعليًا لإعادة النظر في خرائط رُسمت في زمن الاستعمار؟
المؤكد أن المنطقة تدخل مرحلة جديدة تتداخل فيها الجغرافيا بالقانون، والتاريخ بالاستراتيجية، في واحدة من أكثر ملفات القرن الإفريقي حساسية منذ عقود.

Waxaan ka hadlaynaa weerarka joogtada ah iyo aflagaadada uu kula kaco shaqsiyaadka laf-dhabarta u ah Xiriirka Soomaaliyeed ee Kubadda Cagta (XKCS).

Deeq Aariyo ​Maxaa ku qasbaayo, yaana ka dambeeya?

​Inkastoo dhiirigelinta aflagaadada ay inta badan ka timaaddo dano shaqsi ama dad raba inay ragaadiyaan horumarka isboortiga, haddana falkan waxa uu muujinayaa:

​Kalgacal la'aan xirfadeed: In loo adeegsado baraha bulshada duminta sumcadda dadka u taagan adeegga qaranka.

​Saameyn dibadda ah: Marar badan waxaa jira dad daaha nyuma ka taagan oo ka faa'iideysanaya xaaladda dadka qaxootiga ama magangalyo doonka ah si ay ugu fushadaan ujeeddooyin gaar ah.

​Siday u aragtaa Bahda Sports-ku?

​Bulshada isboortiga iyo taageerayaasha kubadda cagtu waxay arrinkan u arkaan:
​Nasiib-darro: In ciyaaryahan hore uu noqdo mid duminaya halkii uu wax dhisid lahaa.

​Anshax-darro: Isboortigu waa akhlaaq iyo is-ixtiraam, aflagaadaduna marna kama mid ahayn dhaqanka ciyaaraha.

​Gabbaad ka dhigasho: In magangalyada lagu jiro loo isticmaalo in lagu waxyeeleeyo dad dalka gudihiisa shaqo adag ka haya waa ficil laga xumaado.

​Farriinteenna:
Waxaan canbaareynaa falalka wuxuush nimo uu kuhaayo bahda sports-ka Deeq Aariyo.

Isboortigu waa midnimo. Waxaan ugu baaqaynaa dhammaan dadka qaybta ka ah bahda sports-ka inay ka fogaadaan wax kasta oo kala qaybin iyo colaad abuuri kara. Shaqsiyaadka laf-dhabarta u ah XKCS waxay mudan yihiin dhiirigelin, haddii dhaliil jirtona waa in loo maraa dariiqooyin saaxiibtinimo iyo mid dhisid ah.

Ecobank Centrafrique a récemment clôturé une formation en leadership numérique et intelligence artificielle. Cette initiative vise à renforcer les compétences des employés de la banque en matière de technologies numériques et d'intelligence artificielle, afin d'améliorer leur efficacité et leur productivité.

La formation a été dispensée par #DHI_Centrafrique et  Ecobank Academy, le centre de formation de la banque, qui propose des programmes de développement des compétences en banque, services financiers numériques et leadership. L'objectif est de doter les employés des compétences nécessaires pour relever les défis de l'économie numérique et contribuer à la croissance de la banque 

Cette initiative s'inscrit dans la stratégie de Ecobank de promouvoir l'inclusion financière et le développement économique en Afrique. La banque a déjà mis en place plusieurs programmes de formation et de développement des compétences pour ses employés à travers le continent

The Philippines dropped six places to 120th out of 182 countries in the 2025 Corruption Perceptions Index (CPI) released by Transparency International, posting a score of 32 out of 100, its lowest rating since 2012.

The CPI measures perceived levels of public sector corruption, with zero considered highly corrupt and 100 seen as very clean. Among Southeast Asian nations, the Philippines ranked higher only than Cambodia and Myanmar.

The decline came amid a multibillion-peso scandal involving alleged anomalies in government flood control projects. Malacañang said the lower score was expected following the administration’s exposure of irregularities, emphasizing that investigations and accountability efforts remain ongoing.

Globally, the CPI average fell to 42, with Transparency International warning that most countries are struggling to control corruption. Denmark, Finland, and Singapore remained among the least corrupt nations, while South Sudan and Somalia ranked at the bottom.

Meanwhile, the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) launched a blockchain-based transparency system called Integrity Chain, aimed at improving monitoring, auditability, and accountability in infrastructure projects.

Officials said the initiative seeks to strengthen safeguards against corruption while promoting transparency in public spending.

🚨UPDATE: DOJ Drops Charges in North Minneapolis ICE Shooting Case

Federal prosecutors have moved to dismiss the criminal complaint against Alfredo Alejandro Aljorna and Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis, citing newly discovered evidence that was described in court filings as “materially inconsistent” with earlier allegations. The dismissal was filed with prejudice, meaning the same charges cannot be brought again.

The case stemmed from a January shooting in north Minneapolis involving an ICE agent. Early public statements from federal officials characterized the incident as an assault on a federal officer. Subsequent court proceedings and reporting revealed disputes over key details, including how the encounter unfolded and what evidence supported the original claims.

According to the government’s motion, the new evidence conflicted with portions of the complaint affidavit and prior testimony. The filing did not publicly detail the nature of that evidence, but the legal effect is clear: the charges are now permanently dismissed!

Renée Good, a 37-year-old poet and mother of three. Alex Pretti, a 37-year-old ICU nurse at a VA hospital—serving our veterans every single day. Both U.S. citizens. Both killed by federal agents during immigration enforcement operations. Both families deserve justice.
I grew up on a multi-generational family farm near Fargo, North Dakota. I was taught that accountability isn't optional—not on the farm, not in our communities, and sure as hell not in our government.

When federal agents deploy 3,000 officers to our state, make 4,000 arrests with ZERO transparency about who was actually a threat, and kill two Americans—including a man who dedicated his life to caring for our veterans—that's not law enforcement. That's chaos.

I don't believe abolishing ICE is the answer. But I do believe in holding every level of government accountable when they fail us.

✅ Transparency — Show us who you arrested and why
✅ Professional standards — Train agents to protect communities, not occupy them
✅ Constitutional rights — Due process matters for everyone
✅ Clear mission — Deport criminals who threaten safety, period.

Immigration enforcement is necessary. But it must be professional, constitutional, and earn the trust of the people it serves.

I'm Bjørn Solberg, and I'm running for Minnesota State Senate District 1 to be a strong voice for Northwest Minnesota in St. Paul—fighting for our family farms, standing up for our veterans, demanding accountability, and pushing back against federal overreach and state neglect.

Our rural communities deserve better. Minnesota deserves better.

ONE HUNDRED AND SEVENTY-FIVE (175) NABBED IN NDOLA CLEAN-UP OPERATION; 17 ETHIOPIAN INTERCEPTED IN SOUTHERN PROVINCE

The Ndola Regional Immigration Office on 13th February, 2026 apprehended one hundred and seventy-five (175) suspected illegal immigrants during a clean-up operation conducted with the support of other security wings. The operation targeted Hill Crest, Mitengo, George, Kawama, Kansenshi and Dola Hill areas of Ndola.

Those apprehended comprised eighty-four (84) Congolese nationals, twenty-six (26) persons whose nationality is yet to be ascertained, seventeen (17) refugees of Congolese origin, eleven (11) Zimbabweans, eight (8) Kenyans, five (5) Somalis, four (4) Tanzanians, four (4) Dutch nationals, four (4) Chinese nationals, three (3) Indians, two (2) Finnish nationals, two (2) refugees of Eritrean origin, one (1) Eritrean, one (1) American, one (1) Lithuanian, one (1) Ghanaian and one (1) refugee of Somali origin.

Following screening and verification processes, eighty-three (83) individuals were released unconditionally after producing documentary proof of lawful presence in the country or confirmation of Zambian nationality. Twenty-five (25) were directed to report to the Ndola Regional Immigration Office on 16th February, 2026 for further verification, while fifteen (15) refugees of Congolese origin were mandated to self-relocate to Meheba Refugee Settlement in line with established refugee management regulations.

Further, ten (10) Congolese adults in the company of twenty-six (26) undocumented minors were physically removed from the country through Sakania Border Control. Six (6) Congolese nationals were served with notices to leave the country for engaging in studies without valid study permits. Two (2) Chinese nationals were released after paying for Temporary Permits to regularise their stay in the country. A Ghanaian national was handed over to the Drug Enforcement Commission for further action. Seven (7) suspects, namely two (2) Chinese and five (5) Congolese nationals, remain in detention pending further investigations and possible prosecution.

Meanwhile, in Southern Province, the Department between 12th and 13th February, 2026 apprehended seventeen (17) Ethiopian nationals for failure to appear before the nearest Immigration Officer on entry into Zambia.

On 13th February, 2026, the Sinazongwe Immigration Office apprehended eleven (11) Ethiopian nationals aged between 17 and 36. Six (6) of them were in possession of valid Ethiopian passports but had no Zambian entry endorsement stamps.

Earlier, on 12th February, 2026, the Kalomo Immigration Office apprehended six (6) Ethiopian nationals aged between 19 and 26, comprising five males and one female, following a tip-off from concerned residents of Kamukeza Village in Zimba District who suspected the presence of prohibited immigrants in their area. The information was also shared with the Zambia Army in Mapatizya. Acting on the information, Immigration Officers working jointly with the Army apprehended the suspects. The six were found with valid passports except for one who was undocumented. Those with passports did not have Zambian entry endorsements and disclosed that they had been abandoned in the bush by unknown individuals while allegedly attempting to transit to South Africa.

The Department of Immigration reiterates that while Zambia remains committed to facilitating lawful travel, trade and regional integration, all foreign nationals are required to comply with immigration laws, including presenting themselves before Immigration Officers upon entry and obtaining appropriate permits for their intended activities. The Department further commends members of the public and cooperating security wings for their continued support and encourages citizens to report suspected immigration violations to the nearest Immigration Office.

Namati H. Nshinka (Mr)
CHIEF PUBLIC RELATIONS OFFICER
255282/0977718155/pro@zambiaimmigration.gov.zm

Tipis stand at Mni Owe Sni (Coldwater Spring), a sacred site for the Dakota and other tribes, across from the Bishop Henry Whipple Federal Building in Minneapolis. Wasuduta (which means Red Hail) said that while some are framing them as activists or protesters, that is not their intent. “This is bigger than that,” said Wasuduta. “This is an actual re-sovereignty claim, because of the atrocities that occurred to my people here.” Indigenous people in the Twin Cities have been detained by ICE at the Whipple Building during Operation Metro Surge. He observed that they are being monitored by federal officials based out of the Whipple Building.

“We’re here because this is who we are,” said Wasuduta. “This is our lands, and it was taken from us. Go in peace in a good way, and I hope that they’ll find it in their hearts to be able to come down here, to have a true understanding of what it means to be a people of the land.” Photos by Aurora Pass

A woman learns that parts of her inner life need protection, and she also learns that some people will try to enter those parts without asking. That’s the situation Warsan Shire describes when she writes about locked rooms inside women and men who arrive either with keys or with hammers. She points out a real problem: women grow up managing desire, grief, and emotional numbness while also managing the risk of intrusion. Some men approach with care and earn trust. Others force their way in through pressure, entitlement, or violence. The difference shapes a woman’s sense of safety in her own body.

Shire, a British Somali poet born in Kenya, has built much of her work around exile, memory, sexuality, and the body. In her poem The House, the metaphor of rooms gives physical form to emotional states that often stay hidden. Lust, grief, apathy. These are not decorative feelings. Lust names appetite and aliveness. Grief names loss and heartbreak. Apathy names shutdown, the dulling that follows repeated hurt. By placing these emotions in domestic rooms, she connects a woman’s inner life to the spaces she’s historically been confined to. The kitchen, the bedroom, the bathroom. These are ordinary places, yet they carry the weight of expectation. Women are expected to cook, to love, to clean themselves up and carry on. Shire shows that inside those same spaces sit hunger, sorrow, and numbness.

When she says men sometimes come with keys, she describes trust earned over time. A key implies consent. Someone has been invited in. The door opens because the woman decides it should. In real life, that looks like a partner who asks before touching, who listens when she says she’s tired, who respects her history instead of demanding access to it. It looks like patience with trauma. It looks like emotional steadiness. A key doesn’t break anything. It fits.

When she says men sometimes come with hammers, she names coercion and force without dressing them up. A hammer doesn’t ask permission. It breaks through. That can mean sexual violence. It can also mean subtler forms of intrusion, such as emotional pressure, guilt, manipulation, or the assumption that intimacy is owed. Many women recognise this pattern. They’ve felt the push to reveal more than they’re ready to share, to forgive before they’ve healed, to provide comfort when they’re depleted. Over time, repeated intrusion can produce apathy, that bathroom of numbness where feeling shuts down because it has cost too much.

The poem also carries generational knowledge. “Mother says” frames the insight as inherited. Mothers teach daughters how to stay safe, how to read men, how to guard themselves. That transmission carries love and fear together. It says the world contains tenderness and danger, and you’ll need to tell the difference. Shire’s own work often draws from stories of displacement and survival within the Somali diaspora, where women navigate cultural expectation alongside personal longing. The locked rooms hold not only individual memory but family history.

Adrienne Rich once wrote about compulsory heterosexuality and the pressure placed on women to organise their lives around men’s needs. Rich argued that women’s emotional and sexual lives often get shaped by expectation rather than choice. Shire’s metaphor gives that argument a body. If a woman’s interior is treated as a house others can enter at will, her own sense of ownership weakens. The locked door becomes an act of self-defence. The key becomes a test of character.

Toni Morrison offers another lens through her portrayal of trauma in Beloved, where memory lives in the body and refuses to disappear. Morrison shows how grief inhabits physical space, how rooms can hold what hasn’t been processed. Shire condenses a similar truth into a few lines. The bedroom of grief isn’t dramatic. It’s recognisable. Many women have loved someone who hurt them. Many have lain awake beside someone and felt alone. Grief can sit next to intimacy without cancelling it.

The power of Shire’s image lies in its honesty about complexity. Women contain desire and pain at the same time. They want connection and they want safety. They can open the door and still fear the sound of splintering wood. The poem doesn’t claim all men carry hammers, yet it refuses to pretend the hammers don’t exist. That refusal is significant because denial leaves women doubting their own experience. Calling out intrusion restores clarity.

In the end, the locked rooms are signs of depth. A person with nothing to guard has nothing precious inside. Warsan Shire insists that a woman’s interior life holds value, and that access to it must be earned. That claim sounds simple, yet in a world where women’s bodies and emotions are often treated as public property, it remains radical.

ZiMoney team Meets with the Somali Ambassador to Australia 🇦🇺🇸🇴

It was a great honour for the Zimoney team to meet with the  Somali Ambassador to Australia & New Zealand H.E Dr Faysal Ahmed Salad and his esteemed delegation.

Our discussion centred on community empowerment, financial inclusion, and strengthening economic partnerships between Australia and Somalia. As a fintech platform focused on cross-border payments and financial accessibility, ZiMoney is committed to playing a meaningful role in supporting diaspora communities and fostering stronger financial connections.

We spoke about:

Empowering the Somali-Australian community through secure and transparent remittance solutions.
Supporting entrepreneurship and small business growth within the diaspora.
Building financial literacy and digital access pathways.
Strengthening long-term trade and investment relationships between both nations.

The Ambassador’s vision for unity, economic development, and collaboration strongly aligns with   ZiMoney's mission, to bridge the global financial divide and empower communities through smarter digital finance.

We look forward to continued engagement and meaningful partnerships that create sustainable impact for our communities.

Happy Birthday to Attorney Inna Simakovsky 🎉👩‍⚖️

Ina, thank you for your courage, your honesty, and your decades of service to immigrant communities here in Columbus and across the United States.

In this episode of Smith Says: Purpose Over Popularity, you didn’t hold back. You shared your journey from arriving in America as a religious refugee from Russia in 1976 to becoming a trusted immigration attorney serving Haitian, Somali, Congolese, Latin American, and many other communities.

You spoke truth about the dangers families face when they trust the wrong people. You educated our community on asylum, TPS, U visas, and T visas. You reminded us why access to real legal help is not a luxury but a necessity. And you showed what it means to continue serving even through burnout, stress, and personal health battles.

Your work has protected families. Your voice has empowered the next generation. Your example is a reminder that purpose is greater than popularity.

On your birthday, we honor you, we thank you, and we celebrate you.

If you or your family need trusted immigration guidance, I encourage you to listen to this powerful conversation and connect with her office.

Historic Voyage: Türkiye’s Deep-Drilling Ship Çağrı Bey Sets Sail for Somalia in Energy Push

Somali Report - Türkiye on Sunday dispatched its newest deep-drilling vessel, Çağrı Bey, to Somalia in what Ankara describes as a historic step in expanding its overseas energy exploration.

The seventh-generation drillship departed from Taşucu Port in the southern province of Mersin during a ceremony attended by Energy and Natural Resources Minister Alparslan Bayraktar and Somali officials. The deployment marks the first time a Turkish drilling ship will operate outside Türkiye’s territorial waters.

“A long way is awaiting Çağrı Bey,” Bayraktar said at the ceremony, noting that the vessel will avoid the Suez Canal and instead sail through the Atlantic Ocean, along the western coast of Africa, before heading toward Mogadishu Port. The journey is expected to take approximately 45 days.

The ship becomes the second Turkish vessel assigned to Somali waters after the seismic research ship Oruç Reis, which was sent from Istanbul in October 2024 and completed its mission last summer.

Ankara and Mogadishu have deepened cooperation in recent years across energy, defense, infrastructure and economic development. Multiple memoranda of understanding have been signed as Türkiye positions itself as one of Somalia’s closest international partners.

The decision to deploy Çağrı Bey comes amid Türkiye’s broader strategy to boost oil and gas exploration both domestically and abroad. Speaking a day earlier in Ankara, Bayraktar described the mission as “another historic step,” signaling that 2026 would be “a year of discoveries and good news.”

He reiterated President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan’s vision of achieving full energy independence for Türkiye. “If Türkiye becomes energy independent, it will be much stronger economically and will have a far stronger treasury,” Bayraktar said.

Domestically, Ankara is intensifying exploration in the Black Sea, where natural gas production is expected to double this year. The government plans new drilling operations off the coasts of Rize, Giresun, Ordu, Samsun and Kastamonu in search of additional reserves.

Bayraktar also highlighted progress at the Akkuyu Nuclear Power Plant, Türkiye’s first nuclear facility currently under construction in Mersin. He said the government aims to begin generating electricity from the plant’s first reactor within the year, with all four reactors eventually supplying up to 10% of the country’s electricity demand.

For Somalia, the arrival of Çağrı Bey signals a new phase in offshore energy exploration that could reshape the country’s economic future. For Türkiye, it underscores Ankara’s ambition to project its energy capabilities beyond its borders — and to secure new partnerships along the way.

The voyage now begins, carrying with it both geopolitical weight and high economic expectations.

Waraysi uu bixiyay wasiirka waxa uu ku sheegay in ciidamada Masar ay qeyb ka yihiin hawlgalka Midowga Afrika ee AUSSOM in ay haddana ka go’an tahay inay door muuqda ka qaataan sidii ay qeyb uga noqon lahaayeen adkeynta amniga iyo xasiloonida Soomaaliya.

Wasiirka Badr oo sii hadlay ayaa sheegay in Masar ay aqoon buuxda u leedahay hawlgallada Nabad ilaalinta sidaas awgeed ay ciidamadooda fahamsan yihiin waxyaabaha waajibka ku ah marka loo diro dalalka u baahan in gacan laga siiyo amniga iyo xasiloonidooda gudaha.

“Masar waxa ay diyaarisay ciidamadii ay u diri lahayd Soomaaliya, ujeedkeenna waa in aan wax kusoo kordhinaa hawlgallada nabad ilaalinta, waxaa naga go’an in aan xoogga saarno adkeynta ammaanka oo aan la shaqeyno ciidamada Soomaalida ah iyo kuwa kale ee Afrika”. – ayuu yirri Wasiirka Arrimaha Dibadda Masar, Badr Abdelatty.

Waxa uu markale adkeeyay in mowqifka Jamhuuriyadda Carbeed Masar ee Soomaaliya uu yahay in aan la kala qeybin oo mid ay tahay Soomaaliya, isaga oo cambaareeyay fargelinta Israa’iil iyo sharci darrada ay kula dhaqaaqday Soomaaliya.

“Mowqifka Masar waa mid marwaliba cad, Soomaaliya waa mid mana jirto sabab loo kala qeybin karo, fargelinta Israa’iil ay ku samaysay waa mid sharciga dhinac marsan, waxaan garab taaganahay Dowladda iyo Shacabka aan walaalaha nahay ee Soomaaliya”- ayuu hadalkiisa sii raaciyay, Wasiir Badr Abdelatty.

Masar ayaa ku biirtay howlgalka Midowga Afrika ee Soomaaliya ka socda, waxa ay dhowaan soo bandhigtay ciidamo wata Gaadiid ka duwan kuwa ay wataan Askarta AUSSOM isla markaana dhoollatus ku hor sameeyay Madaxweyneyaasha Soomaaliya iyo Masar.

Madaxweynaha Jamhuuriyadda Federaalka Soomaaliya, Xasan Sheekh Maxamuud iyo Wasiirka Gaanshaandhigga dowladda Masar Jeneraal Abdel Mageed Saqr ayaa si wadajir ah ugu qeybgalay munaasabad lagu soo bandhigaayay ciidan loo diyaariyay inay ku biiraan hawlgalka Midowga Afrika ee taageerada amniga iyo xasilloonida Soomaaliya ee AUSSOM. 🚨Smuggling Scheme Unraveled — Two Children Safely Recovered by Texas DPS Trooper🚨

2/11/26: A Texas Department of Public Safety - South Texas Region Trooper stopped a black Cadillac XT5 on US-90 in Kinney County and uncovered a human smuggling attempt.

During the stop, the driver was found smuggling four illegal immigrants:

🔹𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐚𝐝𝐮𝐥𝐭 𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐞 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐇𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐮𝐫𝐚𝐬
🔹𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐚𝐝𝐮𝐥𝐭 𝐟𝐞𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐞 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐁𝐞𝐥𝐢𝐳𝐞
🔹𝐓𝐰𝐨 𝐜𝐡𝐢𝐥𝐝𝐫𝐞𝐧, 𝐚𝐠𝐞𝐬 𝟔 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝟖, 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐁𝐞𝐥𝐢𝐳𝐞

Further investigation revealed the male from Honduras paid $10,000 to be smuggled into the U.S. and transported to Florida. The female and two children had been waiting at a stash house for transport to San Antonio.

The driver, Georgina De Hoyos of San Antonio, was arrested and charged with four counts of human smuggling.

📣📣Outstanding investigative work by our trooper — identifying the smuggling operation and ensuring the safe recovery of two young children.📣📣

All four individuals were referred to US Border Patrol custody.

New lawsuit filed against ICE—after little kids forced to run for their lives at raid on "Family Night."

"I didn’t get caught," boy says. "Nobody got some handcuffs on me."

Dozens of minors were among hundreds detained—zip tied and separated from parents.

Agents chose to raid on "Family Night" at La Catedral Arena—a family oriented picnic including children as young as 5 years old.

At one point, a crowd of people looking for their loved ones surged forward, and agents fired rubber bullets at them.

One 8-year-old boy appeared on a local news report and described how he had to pick tiny shards of glass out of his mouth.

The ACLU filed a federal civil rights lawsuit highlighting the mistreatment of families—many of whom were U.S. citizens of Hispanic descent.

The joint FBI and ICE raid was allegedly looking for an illegal gambling operation in Wilder, Idaho. #DemsUnited

🇸🇪İsveç Hükümeti'nden Geri Dönüşleri Artırmak İçin Dev Bütçe

İsveç hükümeti, Suriye, Somali ve Afganistan gibi ülkelere geri dönüşleri artırmak amacıyla BM göç kuruluşu IOM’ye (Uluslararası Göç Örgütü) 70 milyon kron tutarında katkı sağlıyor.

Göç Bakanı Johan Forssell (M), geçen yıl Somali için sağlanan ve tartışmalara yol açan benzer bir desteğin oldukça başarılı sonuçlar verdiğini belirtti. 

Söz konusu ödenek; Irak, Somali, Özbekistan, Afganistan, Suriye, Tunus ve Libya'ya geri dönüşleri teşvik etmek ve düzensiz göçle mücadele etmek amacıyla kullanılacak.

"Sığınmacıların Sadece Üçte Birinin Korunma İhtiyacı Var"

Bakan Forssell, mevcut durumu şu sözlerle değerlendirdi:

"Bugün sığınmacıların sadece üçte birinin gerçek anlamda korunma ihtiyacı bulunuyor. Ancak insanların geri dönmek istememesi ya da geri dönüşün teknik olarak zor olması gibi sorunlarla karşılaşıyoruz."

Forssell, bazı kişilerin ülkelerine kabul edilmediğini veya geçerli kimlik belgelerine sahip olmadığını belirterek şunları ekledi:

"Bu fonlarla, örneğin kişilerin kimliklerini tespit etmek için kapasite oluşturulabilir. Ayrıca döndüklerinde sığınacak bir yer, barınma, gıda ve ilaç gibi temel ihtiyaçların karşılanması da bu kapsamda yer alıyor."

Tartışmalı Somali Anlaşması Gündemde
Geçtiğimiz sonbaharda İsveç radyosu Ekot, İsveç'in Somali'ye yönelik zorunlu sınır dışı işlemlerini artırmak için bu ülkeyle gizli bir anlaşma yaptığını ortaya çıkarmıştı. 

İddialara göre, kalkınma yardımları Somali hükümetine yakın kanallara aktarılmıştı. Dagens Nyheter (DN) gazetesi ise bu fonların bir kısmının çalışmadığı halde maaş alan "hayalet işçilere" gittiğini öne sürmüştü.
Bakan Forssell, bu yolsuzluk iddialarını reddederek şunları söyledi:

"Şu ana kadar hiç kimse yolsuzluk yapıldığına dair somut bir kanıt sunamadı. Birçok iddia ortaya atıldı ama belge yok. Aksine, bu iş birliği sayesinde sınır dışı kararlarının uygulanma oranını kısa sürede beş katına çıkardık."

Suçluların Sınır Dışı Edilmesi Öncelikli
Somali örneğinde olduğu gibi, diğer ülkelerle de kimlik tespiti süreçleri üzerinde duruluyor. Forssell, çalışmanın her ülkede farklı yürütüldüğünü ancak kimlik tespitinin, özellikle suç işleyenlerin sınır dışı edilebilmesi için kritik önemde olduğunu vurguladı.

Bu desteğin bir "şartlı yardım" olup olmadığı sorusuna ise Forssell, paranın doğrudan ülkelere değil IOM üzerinden aktarıldığını belirterek, "Hükümet için geri dönüşlerin başlaması çok net bir önceliktir," dedi.

🚨 CONFRONTATION NEAR MINNEAPOLIS ICE FACILITY CAUGHT ON DASHCAM

What Happened:
Dashcam video circulating online shows a vehicle attempting to leave the area of an anti-ICE protest near a Minneapolis immigration facility when protesters move toward it and spray at the windshield. The driver appears to continue forward as tensions rise. The incident occurred during ongoing demonstrations targeting federal immigration enforcement operations.

Why This Matters:
ICE is a federal agency tasked with enforcing immigration law passed by Congress. Federal facilities are operational sites, not symbolic spaces. When protests escalate into direct confrontation with vehicles or personnel, it creates unnecessary risk and complicates already tense situations.

The Bigger Picture:
Minneapolis has experienced repeated demonstrations related to immigration enforcement since early 2026. Supporters of enforcement argue agents are fulfilling their legal responsibilities and should be allowed to operate without obstruction. As protests intensify, the line between lawful demonstration and unsafe interference becomes increasingly important.

The Takeaway:
Peaceful protest is protected under the Constitution. Obstructing or confronting federal operations can quickly lead to dangerous outcomes and legal consequences.

Shred’s Take:
Border enforcement is not optional policy. It is federal law. Disagreement belongs in elections and legislation, not in physical standoffs outside secure facilities. When protests turn confrontational, it weakens the message and strengthens the case for firmer enforcement.

Dhanaal Furan: Baaq Nabad, Cadaalad iyo Midnimo Qaran.

Dhanbaal furan oo ku socda Dowladda Jamhuuriyadda Somaliland, gaar ahaan Madaxweynaha, hoggaanka qaranka, madaxdhaqameedka, culimada, haweenka, dhalinyarada, iyo aqoonyahanka bulshada.

Waa baaq ku qotoma damiir, dareen qaranimo, iyo rabitaan nabadeed. Waxaa la joogaa xilli loo istaagi lahaa xal u helidda tabasho muddo 30 sano ka badan soo jiitamaysay mudadaas ee ay qabaan   beelaha gobta ah ee Gadabuursi, oo ahna mid jirta oo run ah.

Tabashadaasi maanta waxay maraysaa heer khatar ah — heer ay u eg tahay holac qarxi kara, haddii aan si degdeg ah oo daacad ah wax looga qaban, hadday tahay saami,iyo horumar dhul iyo dadba.

Waa arrin murugo leh in tabashooyinkaas dalal iyo dadka qaarkood ka dhigtaan meel iyo gabood wax laga  dheefsanayo, iyadoo ay u  arkaan xili dadku  jiliceen oo uu jiro niyad jab xoogan gaar ahaan qurba joogta iyo dhalinyarada deegaankaasi iyo meelahaas ka soo jeeda  iyo dil dillaac muuqda wax wada lahaanshihii iyo u sinaantii Somaliland la wada leeyahay. 

Taasi ma aha waddo horseedaysa midnimo, mana aha mid dhisaysa kalsooni qaran & qanaaco bulsho, hadaan cadaalad run ah la helin.

Sidoo kale, colaadda ka aloosan Salal ee bilaha badan jirsatay waxay u baahan tahay in si dhab ah loogu gurmado, loona wajaho wada-hadal, run-sheeg, iyo dib-u-heshiisiin dhab ah. 

Nabadu ma dhalato haddii dhaawac la qariyo; nabadu waxay dhalataa marka caddaalad la helo.

Waxaan soo jeedinayaa in hoggaanka qaranku si toos ah u tagaan Boorama, Awdal, Salal, iyo deegaannada kale ee ay tabashooyinku ka jiraan, lana fadhiistaan bulshada — dhallinyaro, haween, waxgarad iyo odayaal —Madaxdhaqameedka Laguna soo daro Qurbajoogta si xal waara loo helo.

Gargaar degdeg ahna ha la gaadhsiiyo dadka ay saameeyeen colaadaha iyo abaartu; kuwa barakacay, kuwa nugul, iyo kuwa tabaaleysan.

Qaranimada dhabta ahi waa marka la garab istaago kuwa dhibaateysan.

Aragtideennu yeysan ku koobnaan reernimo iyo dano gaaban. Aynu ka gudubno aragti dhow oo ku eeg maanta, una gudubno aragti fog oo badbaadisa berri.

Somaliland mideysan, lagu wada qanacsan yahay, lana wada ilaashanayo, loo wada siman yahay ayaa ina deeqda oo jiri kartana— waa himilada & hiigsi mudan in loo wada istaago sidii loo gaadhi lahaa, waanaynu qaban karnaa hadday Somalilandnimadu dhab inaga tahay.

Maanta haddii aan wax la sixin, berri canaan ayaa ina sugaysa. 

Haddaba, intay goori goor tahay, arrimaha is barkan  si dhab ah ha loo wajaho oo ha la saxo Mudane, Madaxwryne, si aanay u imaan maalin la is eedeeyo.

Tix Gabayga ah aan dhugano walaalayaal 

Qaran jabaa qoomamo dhala,
Qiiro iyo oohin dambaysta,
Haddaan qalbiga la isu furin,
Cadaaladna la quudin.

Midnimo waa geed magangalyo,
Gacmo wada beeray kora,
Haddaynu wada ilaashanno,
Waa hibo aan go’i doonin.

Diyaariye:
Yuusuf Maxamed Cabdi (Yuusuf Qani)
Wasiirkii Hore ee Wasaaradda Maaliyadda J.S.L.

Cawa waxaan rabaa inaan si cad oo aan leexleexad lahayn uga hadlo mowqifkayga, si aan meesha uga saaro hadal heynta iyo dhaliilaha aan sal iyo raad lahayn.

Anigu waxaan si cad u taageeraa dowladnimada, nabadda iyo horumarka dalka ka jira. Taageeradaasi ma aha mid dano shaqsiyeed ku dhisan, mana aha mid kursi siyaasadeed lagu raadinayo. Waa aragti ka timid damiir, waddaniyad iyo faham ku saabsan waxa dal iyo dad lagu dhiso. Dal burbur iyo colaad ku dhisan horumar ma gaaro, bulsho kala qaybsan horay uma socoto, nabad la’aanina mustaqbal ma dhaliso.

Waxaa nasiib darro ah in mar walba la isku dayo in qofka taageera nabadda iyo dowladnimada lagu eedeeyo dano qarsoon ama kursi raadis. Waxaan rabaa inaan cadeeyo: qofka dalkiisa jecel khasab kuma aha inuu xil hayo si uu wanaag u taageero. Sidoo kale khasab kuma aha inuu mucaarad noqdo haddii uusan dowladnimada caayin ama ka hor iman.

Haddii dowlad maanta ka shaqaynayso nabad, xasilooni iyo horumar, taageerideedu ceeb ma aha, waa waajib qaran. Qofka diida wanaagga jira, kana shaqeeya jahawareer iyo kala qaybsanaan, isagaa u baahan inuu dib u fiiriyo waxa uu bulshada u wado.

Anigu waxaan doortay inaan ka mid noqdo dadka jecel wanaagga, midnimada iyo horumarka. Waxaan aaminsanahay in jiilka soo koraya uu u baahan yahay dal nabad ah, dowlad shaqaynaysa iyo bulsho isku kalsoon. Taasi waa waxa aan mar walba garab istaagi doono, habeen iyo maalin.

Cid kasta oo u malaynaysa in mowqifkaygu yahay dano gaar ah ama kursi raadis, waxaan leeyahay: mowqifkaygu waa mid cad, waa mid ku dhisan jacaylka dalka, badbaadada bulshada iyo mustaqbalka ummadda.

Anigu waan ku qanacsanahay dadkayga iyo bulshadayda, waxaana ku faraxsanahay inaan ka mid noqdo dadka ka shaqeeya wanaagga, nabadda iyo horumarka.

— Anas Nuriye Ahmed

Our Wonderful African Family in Full!

I do not know who authored this, but it makes great sense and is something to put a smile on all our faces as Africans!

Read on!

If Africa were an extended family, it could be described as follows:

✍🏾 The Chaotic Relatives:

Uganda🇺🇬, Congo🇨🇩, and South Sudan🇸🇸

They are always wrapped up in some sort of drama. They never fail to be the topic of family gossip. On top of the chaos, Uganda is also a known drunkard.

✍🏾 The Pompous Relatives: 

Kenya🇰🇪 

They believe they know it all, they bully the rest of the family members, and will shout until you declare them the winners of any argument. They are also drunkards like Uganda!

✍🏾 The Peaceful Relatives: 

Tanzania🇹🇿, Zambia🇿🇲, Malawi🇲🇼, Botswana🇧🇼, Gambia🇬🇲, Eswatini🇸🇿, Sierra Leone🇸🇱, and Namibia🇳🇦 

These are soft-spoken and laid back. They are generally sweet to other family members and are not too assertive.

✍🏾 The Disciplined Relatives: 

Rwanda🇷🇼

They are strict and follow rules to the dot. They will not allow any family member to do otherwise in their presence or disrespect them.

✍🏾 The Loud Relatives: 

Ghana🇬🇭 and Nigeria🇳🇬

They go all out on whatever it is they set out to do, good or bad. They were born loud and proud. You will know every little milestone and achievement in their lives whether you have ears or not. They adore each other but act otherwise in public.

✍🏾 The Rich Relatives: 

South Africa🇿🇦

They live in a mansion and never miss an opportunity to flaunt their wealth. Their kids are gifted dancers and singers. Sometimes, other relatives send their children to work for them.

✍🏾 The Distant Relatives: 

Madagascar🇲🇬, Seychelles🇸🇨, Mauritius🇲🇺, Mauritania🇲🇷, Comoros🇰🇲, Cape Verde🇨🇻, Sao Tome and Principe🇸🇹

They live outside the family compound and never attend family events or respond to WhatsApp messages in the family group.

✍🏾 The Private Relatives: 

Chad🇹🇩, Djibouti🇩🇯, Niger🇳🇪, Mali🇲🇱, Benin🇧🇯, Guinea-Bissau🇬🇼, Equatorial Guinea🇬🇶, Guinea🇬🇳, Liberia🇱🇷, and Lesotho🇱🇸

They keep their affairs private. They live by one rule: date in private, propose in private, marry in private, and divorce in private. All their power moves are made in silence. They hardly feature in any family gossip.

✍🏾 The Two-Faced Relatives: 

Egypt🇪🇬, Morocco🇲🇦, Algeria🇩🇿, Tunisia🇹🇳, Libya🇱🇾, Sudan🇸🇩, Ethiopia🇪🇹, Eritrea🇪🇷, and Somalia🇸🇴

They don't want to be associated with the family. They attend family meetings and events, but they also willingly denounce family ties at any given opportunity.

✍🏾 The Abroad Relatives: 

Senegal🇸🇳, Burkina Faso🇧🇫, Mozambique🇲🇿, Angola🇦🇴, Cameroon🇨🇲, Togo🇹🇬, Gabon🇬🇦, Central African Republic🇨🇬, and Ivory Coast🇨🇮

They speak with an accent and prefer to deal with each other since other family members can't relate to their abroad lifestyle.

✍🏾 The Struggling Relatives: 

Zimbabwe🇿🇼

They would be well off, but life has beaten them to a pulp. Things just don't seem to be working out for them, however hard they try.

✍🏾 The Forgotten Relatives:

Burundi 🇧🇮

These are those relatives you don't know wether they are there or not or if they died a long time ago because no one checks on them or even remembers them at all.

Twenty-eight days ago, I launched a small tutoring scholarship fund as my “something small” in the midst of everything going on in the world.

This week, five students begin services.

Two of them are siblings.

Their family recently returned to the U.S. after living abroad for seven years. At home, they speak Somali and English. Both are bright. Both are adjusting. Both needed access to the right support.

Asma is rebuilding reading confidence after a major transition.

Her younger brother, Salman, is creative and endlessly curious — the kind of student who thrives when challenged and understood.

Families don’t all start at the same place. But every child deserves the chance to thrive.

We’ve raised $1,750 of our $3,000 initial goal — and the funds are already being put to work.

More families are waiting.

If you believe financial barriers shouldn’t determine academic opportunity, I’d be grateful if you’d consider:
• Donating
• Sharing
• Or amplifying the link

What started as an idea is now real support for a real family.

**Could Not Detach From My Past**
*Photo Painting*

My story is a complicated one.

I could not merge into a new, foreign culture. I feel as if I am perceived as an alien from a primordial continent — and perhaps I am. A shattered communication can be the culprit of assumptions, misreadings, and silent misunderstandings that widen the distance between worlds.

I believe that my imported culture has no place in my adopted land. It is tethered only to me and my family, carried quietly like an heirloom too fragile to display in public.

In Europe, tribalism is translated differently than our own transcription of it. African culture here is reduced to colorful dressing, drums, rhythmical and chant-like dances, festivity — and, too often, illiteracy. In Europe, tribalism resembles class division, a hierarchy of the poor and the rich. In our hosting country, people gather in churches on Sundays, while in my culture, our community gathers in mosques on Fridays. Yet culture does not necessarily translate into religion, and religion does not capture the full weight of who we are.

Our children navigate this divide more gracefully. Exposed daily to diverse cultural backgrounds through school and sport, they move between worlds with an ease I have not been able to find. I admire them for it.

The unconscious facet of my mind has laid a strong foundation — of culture, belief, and ethics — that is not easily broken, nor should it be entirely. And yet, I confess: I failed to be flexible enough to genuinely understand a foreign culture. I am inclined to believe that my own is better. It does not approve of street drugs, adultery, disrespect toward parents, or the moral erosions I observe around me. These are not simply rules — they are the architecture of a life I was built within.

But I find myself pausing now, brush in hand, image before me.

Should I learn more about their culture? Not to abandon mine, but to truly see — to close the distance between assumption and understanding, between the alien I feel I am and the neighbor I could become.

---

*Mohamed Buwe Osman*

Warbixin ay soo saartay heyadda tirakoobka qaran ee Sweden SCB waxay ka hadlaysaa goorta iyo sababta dhallinyarada Sweden uga guureen guryaha waalidkood intii u dhaxaysay 2014–2024. Waxaa la baarey da’da ragga iyo dumarka ee markii ugu horreeysey guurey, halka ey u guuraan iyo waxa ay sameeyaan sannad kadib guuristooda waxa ey noloshooda sameeyaan.

Waxyaabaha ugu muhiimsan ee warbixintu sheegtay ayaa ah:
Iney gabdhuhu guriga ka guuraan iyaga oo ka da’ yar wiilasha.
Gabdhaha badankood waxey ku guuraan qiyaastii da’da 22-jirka, halka wiilashuna ku guuraan wax yar ka hor da’da 23 jirka.

Kala duwanaansho ayaa jirta ku xiran Meesha uu qofku ku dhashay, qaybta uu dalka ka joogo iyo nooca qoyska uu ka yimid.
Tusaale ahaan, dhallinyarada asal ahaan ka soo jeedda dala dibaddiisa ama ku nool magaalooyinka waaweyn waxey leeyihiin qaabab ka duwan kuwa kale.

Inta badan waxey dhallinyaradaasi u guuraan isla gobolka ey hore u deggenaayeen.
Waxay doorbidaan inay ku nagaadaan isla degmada ama isla gobolka marka ey guriga ka guurayaan.

Sidoo kale waxaa iyaduna kala duwan asbaabaha ey dhallinyaradu guryaha uga guuraan ma la isu barbardhigo hablaha iyo wiilasha.
Hablaha waxay u badan tahay iney u guuraan waxbarasho darteed, halka ey wiilashu u badan yihiin iney shaqo u guuraan.

Dhallinyarada aan la noolayn waalidkood oo is qaba ayaa badanaa guriga ka guura iyaga oo ka da’yar kuwa la nool labadooda waalid oo is qaba.
Weliba gaar ahaan kuwa la nool qoys ka kooban hooyo iyo adeer ama aabbe iyo aayo waxa loo yaqaanno (bonusförälder) waxey guriga ka guuraan iyaga oo da’yar.

Sidoo kale: Ilmaha la nool aabbe keliya waxey guriga ka guuraan waxyar ka hor kuwa la nool hooyadood keliya.
Kuwa ugu da’da yar ee guriga ka guura waa kuwa la nool aabbahood iyo aayadood (naagta aabbahood uu qabo).
Warbixintu waxay adeegsatay xog rasmi ah oo ka socota diiwaanada SCB.
Waxay ku salaysan tahay diiwaanka dadweynaha iyo guuritaanka rasmiga ah ee qofka ugu horreeya.

Gedeng Sekotong dan Etika Kedermawanan: Ketika Elit Sosial Membumi

Di tengah realitas sosial yang sering memperlihatkan jarak antara kaum berada dan masyarakat biasa, di balik dua gerbang Gedeng Sekotong,  dihadirkan gambaran berbeda. Sebagai tempat orang kaya dan terhormat, ia tidak menutup diri dalam lingkaran eksklusif, di dalamnya ada dapur umum yang yang setia mengabdi kepada setiap orang yang singgah dan memamsuki tempat itu, bergaul langsung dengan masyarakat tanpa sekat sosial yang kaku.
Secara sosiologis, tindakan ini bukan sekadar amal, tetapi praktik sosial yang sarat makna.

1. Solidaritas Sosial (Emile Durkheim)

Menurut Emile Durkheim, masyarakat bertahan karena adanya solidaritas. Dalam masyarakat tradisional, solidaritas bersifat mekanik—terbangun dari rasa kebersamaan dan nilai yang sama. Membuka dapur umum adalah bentuk konkret solidaritas itu: memperkuat ikatan kolektif, mengurangi jarak sosial, dan meneguhkan rasa “kita” dalam komunitas.
Gedeng Sekotong, sebuah manifestasi nyata yang berinteraksi langsung dengan setiap orang, bukan lagi tingkat lokal, bahkan sampai orang luar pulau yang kemudian memperkuat kohesi sosial. Ia tidak sekadar memberi, tetapi hadir. Dalam perspektif Durkheimian, kehadiran itu jauh lebih penting daripada sekadar distribusi materi.

2. Tindakan Sosial (Max Weber)

Max Weber membagi tindakan sosial ke dalam beberapa tipe, salah satunya tindakan berorientasi nilai (wertrational). Jika kedermawanan dilakukan karena keyakinan moral atau etika kemanusiaan, maka itu termasuk tindakan rasional berorientasi nilai—bukan demi keuntungan pribadi, melainkan karena dianggap benar secara etis. Dalam hal ini, dapur umum Gedeng Sekotong bukan hanya tentang makanan dan perut,tetapi ekspresi nilai: kepedulian, tanggung jawab sosial, dan kesadaran akan posisi sebagai bagian dari masyarakat, bukan di atas masyarakat. Gedeng Sekotong memahami bahwa legitimasi bukan dengan kekuasaan simbolik yang berjarak, melainkan dengan empati yang membumi.

3. Patronase Tradisional dan Transformasinya

Dalam starata sosial masyarakat , figur kaya dan terpandang sering berperan sebagai patron—pelindung dan penanggung jawab kesejahteraan komunitas. Namun patronase yang sehat bukanlah dominasi, melainkan relasi timbal balik berbasis penghormatan.
Gedeng Sekotong memperlihatkan transformasi patronase ke arah yang lebih egaliter: ia tetap dihormati, tetapi tidak menciptakan jarak simbolik. Ia menjaga kehormatan tanpa kehilangan kedekatan.

Kedermawanan Keluarga besar Gedeng Sekotong mengajarkan bahwa kehormatan sejati tidak lahir dari jarak, tetapi dari kedekatan. Kekayaan tidak harus membangun tembok sosial; ia bisa menjadi jembatan.
Dalam perspektif ilmu sosial, tindakan membuka dapur umum, menyantuni anak yatim, bakti sosial dan sebagainya adalah praktik solidaritas, pembangunan modal sosial, dan tindakan moral berorientasi nilai. Ia membuktikan bahwa menjadi orang kaya dan terhormat tidak berarti harus eksklusif—justru kebesaran seseorang tampak ketika ia tetap membumi bersama masyarakatnya. 

Sehat selalu Keluarga Besar Gedeng Sekotong. Jika ada yang tak suka, mungkin karena mereka belum pernah duduk dan makan bersama di berugak, sambil memikirkan berapa biaya yang keluar selama bertahun tahun dengan adanya dapur umum ini yang tak memandang kasta

every year that passes, i realize what a gift ive been given to still be alive. it’s a miracle that i ever even drew a sober breath. even if it stopped right there - that would have been a miracle in itself. so many people don’t make it. including friends and loved ones of mine. and i did. and i wasn’t even trying. there was no “quitting”. i was absolutely incapable on my own power of stopping even if i wanted to. i was sure i would die sooner than later and maybe then, the nightmare would be over.

all credit goes to God. He absolutely did for me what i could not do for myself. i don’t question it - i try to make the best out of it and use my pain and experiences to help other people. there is no drug in the world that compares to when after hours, days, years of me working on something, pouring my soul unfiltered - no writing, sacrificing time for myself, my wife, my family, etc and i see you guys listen and you get it. you understand me. you are me, only i just have a big loud microphone. you cry. you light up and turn the fuck up. and for that moment - we connect. there is no better feeling than that and yall give me that. year after year. i could never repay what all of you have given me.

i know what it’s like to feel hopeless. to see other people get sober, change, etc and be like “fuck them”. idc if you’re sober, what religion or politics you subscribe to, if you’re shooting fent, I LOVE YOU. you’re not alone. you have a chance. i didn’t think any of that applied to me. good for other people but wouldn’t work for me. i’ve never been more wrong in my life. if you need help - please reach out and get it. it’s not fuckin easy. but it really is simple. if you can get honest, have an open mind that maybe you don’t know everything you think you know, and are just the tiniest bit willing to do something different - you have a shot

i used to think i didn’t believe in anything. i was a nihilistic miserable mother fucker. life had no meaning. there was no higher power. life was meant to just be tolerated at best. how wrong i was. even when i was a “lazy atheist” i was worshipping something and didn’t even know it - I WAS WORSHIPPING MY THINKING AND MY REASONING. i worshipped praise. money. fame. validation. music. so i did have faith all along. only that faith was in a delusional idiot who thought he was smart (me). but once i put my life into the hands of something bigger (whatever was working for my sponsor or the old timers in AA cuz i was incapable of comprehending a god) my life changed. i started to stay sober. i wasn’t obsessing about getting high. my perspective of the world changed by working a couple stupid steps.

some people say i talk to much on here. i care too much about what yall think. i lose the “mystique” by spilling and talking to yall like a normal person. but FUCK THAT. i am a normal person. i’m not special. God has given me a gift and today i pray im using it to help whoever is willing to listen. i’m not perfect. im broken. i’m a boy who is scared of the world although i act as if i fear nothing. i’m littered with fear. drugs were my way to turn the volume down. instant acceptance every time i got high. fuck being sober - wanted nothing to do with it. i rather die.

thank you God. thank you 12 step programs. thank you to getting back to my faith in my Lord, Jesus Christ. thank you to anyone and everyone who stuck with me and even the ones that didn’t. i truly understand. thank you to my wife. she is the best person i’ve ever met. my parents who tried and continue to try their best. broken for sure but always loved me with everything in them. thank you guys for giving me a life i couldn’t even fathom and getting to do what i love every fuckin day. even if this helps one person - then great. today is not about me - it’s about showing there is hope, God is real, and you have a chance. please don’t give up.

Your support doesn’t just help me — it *motivates* me. It keeps me grounded, focused, and aligned. And no matter how far I go or how much I grow, I’ll always remember the people who helped me build the foundation. The ones who cheered when no one else was clapping. The ones who showed up before the world was watching.

So here’s to you — my silent supporters, my loyal followers, my quiet cheerleaders, my loud encouragers, and everyone in between.

Thank you. For everything.

I see you. I appreciate you. I value you. And I’m grateful for you .

อ่านสถานการณ์คอร์รัปชันโลกผ่านดัชนี CPI 2025: ความเสี่ยง โอกาส และทิศทางการต่อต้านคอร์รัปชัน 

ดัชนีภาพลักษณ์คอร์รัปชัน (Corruption Perceptions Index: CPI) เป็นหนึ่งในดัชนีที่ได้รับการยอมรับอย่างกว้างขวางในระดับสากลสำหรับประเมินระดับการรับรู้การทุจริตของประเทศต่าง ๆ ทั่วโลก จัดทำโดย Transparency International เป็นประจำทุกปี ดัชนีนี้ไม่เพียงสะท้อนภาพลักษณ์ของความโปร่งใสในการใช้อำนาจรัฐ หากยังทำหน้าที่เป็นสัญญาณชี้วัดคุณภาพของสถาบัน กลไกตรวจสอบ และธรรมาภิบาลของแต่ละประเทศ การทำความเข้าใจกรอบแนวคิดและวิธีการประเมินของ CPI จึงมีความสำคัญอย่างยิ่งต่อการวิเคราะห์สถานการณ์คอร์รัปชัน และการออกแบบแนวทางยกระดับการต่อต้านคอร์รัปชันให้มีประสิทธิผลอย่างแท้จริง  
. 
KRAC Extract ประจำเดือนนี้จะพาไปเจาะลึกข้อมูลจากรายงาน Corruption Perceptions Index 2025 เพื่อทำความเข้าใจแนวโน้มสำคัญของการทุจริตในระดับโลก วิเคราะห์ความเชื่อมโยงกับประชาธิปไตย เสรีภาพภาคประชาชน ระบบยุติธรรม และคุณภาพบริการสาธารณะ ตลอดจนชี้ให้เห็นความท้าทายใหม่ ๆ ที่กำลังทดสอบความเข้มแข็งของสถาบันรัฐในหลายประเทศ  
. 
ดัชนี CPI ปี 2025 ประเมิน 182 ประเทศ โดยให้คะแนนตั้งแต่ 0 (การทุจริตสูงมาก) ถึง 100 (โปร่งใสสูงมาก) ค่าเฉลี่ยของโลกอยู่ที่ 42 คะแนนจาก 100 ซึ่งนับเป็นการปรับลดลงครั้งแรกในรอบกว่าทศวรรษ ที่สำคัญ สองในสามของประเทศทั้งหมดมีคะแนนต่ำกว่า 50 บ่งชี้ว่าการทุจริตยังคงเป็นปัญหาเชิงระบบในหลายภูมิภาค ขณะเดียวกัน จำนวนประเทศที่ได้คะแนนสูงกว่า 80 ลดลงเหลือเพียง 5 ประเทศ จากเดิม 12 ประเทศเมื่อสิบปีก่อน สะท้อนแนวโน้มถดถอยของมาตรฐานความโปร่งใสในภาพรวม 
. 
เดนมาร์กยังคงครองอันดับสูงสุดด้วยคะแนน 89 ต่อเนื่องเป็นปีที่แปด แสดงถึงความเข้มแข็งของสถาบันรัฐ กลไกตรวจสอบ และวัฒนธรรมทางการเมืองที่ยึดมั่นในหลักความรับผิดชอบ ในขณะเดียวกัน ประเทศที่รั้งท้ายตาราง ได้แก่ โซมาเลียและเซาต์ซูดาน ซึ่งได้คะแนนเพียง 9 คะแนน ต่างก็เผชิญกับปัญหาความขัดแย้ง ความอ่อนแอของสถาบัน และการกดขี่ทางการเมืองอย่างต่อเนื่อง ส่วนประเทศที่มีคะแนนต่ำกว่า 25 ส่วนใหญ่อยู่ในบริบทของความเปราะบางทางสถาบันและระบบกฎหมายที่ไม่สามารถคุ้มครองความเป็นธรรมได้อย่างมีประสิทธิภาพ 
. 
ข้อมูลเชิงเปรียบเทียบตามประเภทระบอบการปกครองชี้ให้เห็นความสัมพันธ์อย่างมีนัยสำคัญระหว่างระดับประชาธิปไตยกับการควบคุมการทุจริต ประเทศที่อยู่ในกลุ่ม “Full democracies” มีคะแนน CPI เฉลี่ย 71 คะแนน ขณะที่ “Flawed democracies” มีค่าเฉลี่ย 47 และ “Non-democratic regimes” มีค่าเฉลี่ยเพียง 32 คะแนน ข้อค้นพบดังกล่าวตอกย้ำว่าการมีสถาบันอิสระ การเลือกตั้งที่เสรีและเป็นธรรม กลไกถ่วงดุลอำนาจ และการตรวจสอบจากสาธารณะ เป็นปัจจัยสำคัญในการลดความเสี่ยงด้านการทุจริต 
. 
อย่างไรก็ตาม แนวโน้มที่น่าวิตกคือการถดถอยของบางประเทศที่เคยมีคะแนนสูงและได้รับการยอมรับด้านธรรมาภิบาล ประเทศอย่างสหรัฐอเมริกา แคนาดา สหราชอาณาจักร ฝรั่งเศส สวีเดน และนิวซีแลนด์ มีแนวโน้มคะแนนลดลงในช่วงทศวรรษที่ผ่านมา โดยมีเหตุปัจจัยสำคัญ ได้แก่ การอ่อนแอลงของกลไกตรวจสอบภายในรัฐ ช่องว่างด้านกฎหมายการเงิน การเมือง และอิทธิพลของทุนเอกชนต่อกระบวนการกำหนดนโยบาย การเปลี่ยนแปลงดังกล่าวสะท้อนว่า ความโปร่งใสไม่ใช่สิ่งที่ยั่งยืนโดยตัวมันเอง หากปราศจากการดูแลและปกป้องอย่างต่อเนื่อง ก็อาจถดถอยลงได้ 
. 
อีกประเด็นสำคัญที่รายงานเน้นย้ำคือความสัมพันธ์ระหว่างการเปิดกว้างของพื้นที่ภาคประชาชน (civic space) กับระดับการทุจริต ประเทศที่คุ้มครองเสรีภาพในการแสดงความคิดเห็น การรวมกลุ่ม และการดำเนินงานของภาคประชาสังคม มีคะแนน CPI สูงกว่าประเทศที่จำกัดเสรีภาพดังกล่าวอย่างมีนัยสำคัญ ในช่วงตั้งแต่ปี 2012 เป็นต้นมา 36 จาก 50 ประเทศที่คะแนนลดลงอย่างมีนัยสำคัญ มีแนวโน้มการหดตัวของเสรีภาพพลเมืองควบคู่กันไป การจำกัดการทำงานขององค์กรภาคประชาสังคม การออกกฎหมายควบคุมองค์กรอิสระ และการคุกคามสื่อมวลชน ล้วนเป็นปัจจัยที่บั่นทอนความสามารถของสังคมในการตรวจสอบการใช้อำนาจรัฐ 
. 
รายงานยังระบุว่า ตั้งแต่ปี 2012 มีนักข่าวอย่างน้อย 150 คนถูกสังหารจากการรายงานข่าวที่เกี่ยวข้องกับการทุจริต และกว่าร้อยละ 90 ของเหตุการณ์ดังกล่าวเกิดขึ้นในประเทศที่มีคะแนน CPI ต่ำกว่า 50 สถานการณ์ดังกล่าวสะท้อนว่าการคุ้มครองสื่อและผู้เปิดโปงการทุจริตเป็นองค์ประกอบสำคัญของระบบธรรมาภิบาล หากปราศจากสื่ออิสระและภาคประชาชนที่เข้มแข็ง โอกาสในการเปิดเผยและแก้ไขการทุจริตย่อมลดลงอย่างมีนัยสำคัญ 
. 
ในมิติของระบบยุติธรรม รายงานชี้ให้เห็นว่า ความเป็นอิสระของศาลและความพร้อมด้านงบประมาณ บุคลากร และศักยภาพในการทำงาน มีความสัมพันธ์โดยตรงกับระดับการทุจริต เมื่อการแต่งตั้งผู้พิพากษาและอัยการถูกแทรกแซง หรือการดำเนินคดีอยู่ภายใต้แรงกดดันทางการเมือง ความน่าเชื่อถือของกฎหมายย่อมสั่นคลอน ขณะเดียวกัน หากกระบวนการยุติธรรมมีต้นทุนสูง ล่าช้า หรือเข้าถึงได้ยาก ก็ยิ่งเปิดช่องให้เกิดการติดสินบนและการใช้อำนาจโดยมิชอบ 
. 
ผลกระทบของการทุจริตยังปรากฏชัดในภาคบริการสาธารณะ เมื่อเงินงบประมาณบริหารจัดการอย่างไม่โปร่งใส ประชาชนอาจถูกจำกัดการเข้าถึงบริการขั้นพื้นฐาน เช่น สาธารณสุข การศึกษา น้ำ และไฟฟ้า รายงานชี้ว่าประเทศที่มีคะแนน CPI สูง มักมีคุณภาพและความครอบคลุมของบริการสุขภาพที่ดีกว่า ในทางตรงกันข้าม ประเทศที่มีระดับการทุจริตสูงมักเผชิญความเหลื่อมล้ำทางสังคมที่รุนแรง และประชาชนอาจต้องเผชิญค่าใช้จ่ายสูงซึ่งกระทบผู้มีรายได้น้อยอย่างไม่เป็นธรรม 
. 
นอกจากนี้ รายงานยังให้ความสำคัญกับมิติของการทุจริตข้ามพรมแดน แม้ดัชนี CPI จะเน้นการรับรู้การทุจริตภายในประเทศ แต่เงินที่ได้จากการทุจริตมักถูกโอนและซ่อนในศูนย์การเงินระหว่างประเทศ การฟอกเงินและการซ่อนทรัพย์สินที่ได้มาโดยมิชอบ ทำให้ประเทศต้นทางสูญเสียทรัพยากรที่จำเป็นต่อการพัฒนา การแก้ไขปัญหาดังกล่าวจำเป็นต้องอาศัยความร่วมมือระหว่างประเทศ การบังคับใช้กฎหมายที่มีประสิทธิภาพ และกลไกคืนทรัพย์สินอย่างโปร่งใส 
. 
แม้ภาพรวมจะสะท้อนแนวโน้มถดถอย แต่รายงานยังระบุกรณีของประเทศที่สามารถปรับปรุงคะแนนได้อย่างต่อเนื่อง อาทิ ประเทศที่ดำเนินการปฏิรูปเชิงโครงสร้าง เสริมสร้างความเป็นอิสระของหน่วยงานตรวจสอบ ปรับปรุงกฎหมายการเงินการเมือง และนำเทคโนโลยีดิจิทัลมาใช้ในการบริหารภาครัฐ แนวทางดังกล่าวสะท้อนว่าการลดการทุจริตไม่ใช่เรื่องเกินเอื้อม หากมีเจตจำนงทางการเมืองที่ชัดเจนและเปิดพื้นที่ให้สังคมมีส่วนร่วมอย่างแท้จริง 
. 
โดยสรุป ดัชนี CPI 2025 ทำหน้าที่เป็นเครื่องมือสำคัญในการประเมินสภาพธรรมาภิบาลของโลกในช่วงเวลาที่เปราะบาง ความเข้มแข็งของประชาธิปไตย การคุ้มครองเสรีภาพพลเมือง และความเป็นอิสระของสถาบันตรวจสอบ เป็นองค์ประกอบหลักในการควบคุมการทุจริต การถดถอยในองค์ระกอบเหล่านี้ย่อมเพิ่มความเสี่ยงต่อการใช้อำนาจโดยมิชอบ 

. 
ในสภาวการณ์ที่โลกเผชิญความท้าทายเชิงโครงสร้างหลายประการ ความโปร่งใสและความรับผิดชอบจึงไม่ใช่เพียงหลักการที่พูดถึงกันในเชิงอุดมคติ หากเป็นรากฐานสำคัญของเสถียรภาพทางเศรษฐกิจ ความยุติธรรมทางสังคม และการพัฒนาอย่างยั่งยืน การเสริมสร้างกลไกตรวจสอบ การคุ้มครองสื่อและภาคประชาชน และการส่งเสริมความร่วมมือระหว่างประเทศ จึงเป็นภารกิจเร่งด่วนที่ทุกภาคส่วนต้องร่วมกันขับเคลื่อน เพื่อปกป้องผลประโยชน์สาธารณะและอนาคตของสังคมโดยรวม

Maanta waa maalin Jimco ah, waana bishii barakeysnayd ee Ramadaan. Waa laba fadli oo waaweyn oo is biirsaday: fadliga Jimcaha iyo fadliga Soonka. Qofkii ka faa’iidaysta maanta, wuxuu helayaa kheyr aad u badan.

Alle Subxaanahu watacaalaa wuxuu Qur’aanka ku yiri:

“إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ وَمَلَـٰٓئِكَتَهُۥ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى ٱلنَّبِىِّ ۚ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ صَلُّوا۟ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا۟ تَسْلِيمًا” (Suuratul Axzaab: 56)

Aayaddan waxay ina faraysaa in aan ku sallino Nabigeenna Muxamed ﷺ, maadaama Alle iyo malaa’igtiisuba ku salliyaan. Haddaba maxaa ka fadli badan in Jimco iyo Ramadaan la isku daro, laguna badiyo saliga iyo salaanta Nabiga ﷺ?

Sidoo kale Alle wuxuu ka yiri Jimcaha:
“يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا نُودِىَ لِلصَّلَوٰةِ مِن يَوْمِ ٱلْجُمُعَةِ فَٱسْعَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ وَذَرُوا۟ ٱلْبَيْعَ” (Suuratul Jumu’ah: 9)

Tani waxay muujinaysaa qiimaha iyo karaamada maalinta Jimcaha. Waa maalin cibaado, xus iyo u soo noqosho Alle.

Soonkuna waa gaashaan, sida uu Nabigu ﷺ yiri: “Soonku waa gaashaan.” Ramadaantuna waa bil Qur’aan:

“شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أُنزِلَ فِيهِ ٱلْقُرْءَانُ” (Suuratul Baqarah: 185)

Waa bil naxariis, dambi dhaaf iyo xoreynta Naarta.
Haddaba maanta oo Jimco ah, adigoo sooman, ku dadaal: – Inaad badiso saliga Nabiga ﷺ
Inaad akhrido Qur’aan Inaad ducada badiso, gaar ahaan saacadda la aqbalo

Inaad sadaqo bixiso iyo camal kheyr ah samayso
Aan carrabkeenna ka dhigno mid qoyan oo leh: Allahuuma salli calaa Muxamed wa calaa aali Muxamed.

Ilaahay ha inaga aqbalo soonkeena, salaaddeena, saligeena iyo camalladeenna oo dhan, hana inaga dhigo kuwa ka faa’iidaysta Jimcaha iyo Ramadaanta si buuxda. Aamiin.
